title: Deterministic replay — replay driver
purpose: The replay driver replays a recorded event log by feeding recorded decisions back at each non-deterministic decision point. During replay, selective receive returns the recorded message, timers fire at recorded times, and NIF calls return recorded results.
task: Implement the replay driver that replays from a recorded event log.

requirements.title: Replay driver
requirements.spec: A driver that feeds recorded events back at decision points during replay.
acceptance: At each decision point, the driver returns the recorded event instead of the live decision
Selective receive returns the message at the recorded index
Timer expiry is replayed at the recorded time
NIF calls return the recorded result (NIF is NOT re-executed)
Test: record a run, replay it, verify identical execution trace

requirements.title: Replay mode on scheduler
requirements.spec: The scheduler can be started in replay mode with a recorded log.
acceptance: Scheduler::new_replay(config, log) creates a replay scheduler
Replay mode disables real I/O, timers, and random — all from the log
Execution is deterministic: same log produces identical behavior every time
Test: replay same log twice, verify identical outcomes

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement partial replay (replay from a checkpoint) — full replay only
SHALL NOT modify the recorded log during replay
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
